title: Bytes, Text I/O, Encodings, Newlines, and Cleanup · Python Build Room
summary: A library-tools pair incrementally builds explicit text-I/O boundary from an original fixed text fixture while retaining earlier Git, Python, Unicode, determinism, bounded-resource, accessibility, testing, and evidence requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of explicit text-I/O boundary while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish raw bytes, decoded str values, code points, grapheme clusters, and serialized output at every boundary.
Choose and document UTF-8 or another declared encoding instead of relying on locale-dependent defaults.
Select strict rejection, replacement, ignore, or surrogate handling from the stakeholder evidence duty and expose data loss.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Bytes Text Boundary
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Bookbot team handles case 2425 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to decode bytes under an explicit UTF-8 and cleanup contract without hiding malformed input; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish raw bytes, decoded str values, code points, grapheme clusters, and serialized output at every boundary.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Bytes Text Boundary.

Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-context-cleanup-1m2whc
# Competency: bookbot-context-cleanup.
# Case 4277: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def evidence_bookbot_context_cleanup_1m2whc(byte_count: int = 120, decoded_characters: int = 112, encoding: str = "utf-8", decode_errors: int = 0, handle_closed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1m2whc = "1m2whc-chw2m1-4277"
    assert evidence_variant_1m2whc
    evidence_axes_1m2whc = (
        "corpus1m2whc",
        "policychw2m1",
        "revision4277",
        "command1m2whc4277",
        "reportchw2m14277",
        "test42771m2whc",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1m2whc)) == 6
    if byte_count < 0 or decoded_characters < 0:
        return False, byte_count, "io-count"
    if encoding.casefold() != "utf-8":
        return False, decoded_characters, "encoding-policy"
    if decode_errors > 0:
        return False, decode_errors, "decode-rejected"
    if not handle_closed:
        return False, byte_count, "cleanup"
    assert byte_count >= decoded_characters
    return True, decoded_characters, "decoded-text"
